title: Superdrug: What Do You Think Of Botox On The High Street？
filename: Superdrug： What Do You Think Of Botox On The High Street？.en.vtt
excerpt: Hello again everyone I'm really sorry I deleted that video by mistake when I was closing my browser in a rush so here I am doing again and I'm doing a podcast later today and the focus is basically Botox on the high street or the super drug thing so as most of you probably aware …

segments.text: hello again everyone I'm really sorry I deleted that video by mistake when I was closing my browser in a rush so here I am doing again and I'm doing a podcast later today and the focus is basically Botox on the high street or the super drug thing so as most of you probably aware super drug announced that they're starting doing an aesthetic procedures in their pharmacies and the question is what you guys think about this is this gonna make medical esthetics worse than it is already is it going to make it better and is it something that you immediately had a gut reaction to I are you afraid of it as a clinic owner do you think this is gonna make a threat to your business and/or do you think it's just basically growing the market and the more the merrier and everyone join in because the market will differentiate into different segments anyway and not everyone's going to go to Superdrug but they may think about it more and then maybe they'll come to your practice do you think it's making it a bit like some people feel a bit like

it's like McDonald's doing aesthetic medicine which it would just makes it too accessible or too frivolous and so I'm really interested to hear your opinions on that and sorry if you really what I'm seeing people join is for the other one I'm sorry I deleted the video by mistake Andy but it's the same question and which is what do you think about basically big high street brands doing procedures and in-house basically would it be different if it was John Lewis would it be different if it was boots even and because I know both basically both of those already do procedures I think John Doe format might be getting that confused with one of those other big places in the traffic center but certainly boots of an app have announced it and then gone back from at various times so really interested to hear your views thank you've got one already out and then this other question about offering 99 pounds per for two areas as well which is an interesting statement I don't know that's gonna be difficult I'll discuss that in particular on the podcast because it that there are the

one thing I can tell you about bigger businesses is that they are much more compliant than smaller businesses so it becomes they if they're going to cut corners like they have to cut corners properly you can't cut corners and not do things by the book it's much harder the bigger you get because people are always looking at you basically so I'll talk about that on the podcast as well the other big thing is complications complications it's definitely finally got a lot more attention we have a complications course starting and I'm also doing an e-learning complications course so I'm constantly looking for questions and that particularly are of interest to you with regard to complications today I'm focusing a little bit more on Botox so if you have difficult cases if you've had droopy eyes drew before heads droopy cheeks droopy lips and puffiness flu-like symptoms I'm really interested to hear the type of complications that you've dealt with and where you find a tricky where you'd like a bit more advice any

questions on how to avoid complications had to reduce the risk of it and maybe you don't even understand exactly what it is that's causing the problem I know with eyelid ptosis it's more it's more complicated than you think it's not just getting a little bit of Botox near your eye and it can affect all sorts of other structures around the eye so we'll go into more detail I'm certainly doing that on my elearning platform and what we're doing a lot more detail on exactly which muscles are affected which ones are near to each other and really neatly relating your injection technique to how to reduce side effects and risks so and other questions on that would be great and obviously we produce loads of free content is not all gonna go into the courses that we do online but so but I but I'd love to hear actually what people are facing day to day because you can google it and find out you know what the side effects and risks are but that's very different to what it how it appears in your clinic so it would be great to hear you know how do patients describe the problem I know one of the things which I realized quite sooner and as patients through the word stroke in

very early like then I look like I've had a stroke is actually that you have slightly more movement on one side than the other and they phrase it in this terrifying way which just like ruins your day until you see them and then you realize oh you just need a little tweak so what is it how do these side-effects follow-ups come back to your clinic how are you handling them how would you like to get better and are there any areas where you wish you knew a bit more about it is it to do with the words that used to explain to your patients what's going on or is it that you don't understand how to reduce the risk or you don't know how to select for certain patients to avoid the risk and that would be great so today Botox questions and I'll try and answer them all overtime as well on this thread but love to hear from you and then also we'll be announcing the date for our complications course very soon you actually have three things going on there's a cadaver course which is going to happen less frequently where we're going to look at complications from dermal fillers that's that actually does have a date but I bloody well can't remember it and I'll post that on here

as well then we're going to do a more regular complications course that will be based probably in our clinic with dr. Amy and dr. our med leading that then we have an e-learning module which is going to cover in kind of huge kind of very academic level detail on exactly what I'll give you an idea like yeah just to give you an idea of the scale of it droopy eyelid I mean you could explain everything you need to do in maybe a paragraph but by the time I'd finished writing the script for yesterday's two and a half thousand words long and that's not even including management so I do want it to be like encyclopedic in terms of the end result but I do need help with that actually to actually get my head around the day-to-day problems that you're having so I can answer them in a really practical way not just theoretical or practical so that you actually know how to handle it so anyway that's my call for a little bit of help any questions you can inbox me if you don't know make it public you can send me pictures if you don't have permission to make them public and you want advice on them I have an idea about how I could use

pictures that without actually making them which is basically to make maybe like a black and white image and illustrate the curves I don't know how well that'll work but obviously you can't always use all the patient data but um but in a way that people understand the concept I might be able to do that and because that's one of the hardest things with complications is you don't ever get to see them in your practice if you're a reasonably good practitioner even reasonably good not even the best will not see very many of them and if you get very good at it you can go years without seeing a complication like certainly a serious one if not like a decade so how do you get practiced at it well that's one of the things like we need to share and talk more I'm a big supporter of groups like the ACE group where people actually go on and share their complications other Facebook groups loads of them I know it's a touchy subject but you know I think we've got to get the information out there that so that people get better so share your stories ask me questions I will formulate it into a course various

courses and I'll answer them on here so that we all get better together so thank you very much for watching so if you saw the earlier one I said more on this one anyone this one was better so but hopefully if you listen to a podcast I will read your questions out as well and your opinions about Superdrug and I will also answer your questions on complications over time thank you all very much for watching see you again next time
